title: Bubble and Squeak

overview.description: A traditional British dish made from leftover mashed potatoes and cooked vegetables, fried until crisp and golden, then served with a fried egg.

ingredients: 400g leftover cooked cabbage, sprouts or other green vegetables, chopped
method: Heat the butter and vegetable oil in a large frying pan over a medium heat.
Cook the onion for 5–6 minutes until softened.
Mix the mashed potatoes and chopped vegetables together.
Season with salt and black pepper.
Add the mixture to the frying pan and press it down into an even layer.
Cook without stirring for 8–10 minutes until the underside is crisp and golden.
Carefully turn the mixture over in sections and cook for a further 8–10 minutes.
Meanwhile, fry the eggs to your preferred level of doneness.
Serve topped with a fried egg.

learning.did-you-know: Bubble and squeak gets its name from the sounds made as the vegetables cook in the frying pan.
learning.what-you-will-learn: How to transform leftovers into a new meal and achieve a crisp golden crust without the mixture falling apart.

notes.why-its-in-the-chapman-kitchen: One of the best examples in the cookbook of making something delicious from leftovers while reducing food waste.
chapman-tips: Cold mashed potatoes hold together much better than freshly made mash.
Don't move the mixture around too much—leave it to develop a crisp crust.
Almost any cooked vegetables work well, especially cabbage, sprouts, carrots and peas.
A cast iron frying pan gives the best crust.
notes.batch-cooking: Not usually batch cooked, but ideal for using leftovers from a roast dinner.
notes.leftovers: Reheat in a frying pan until crisp again.
